item: reading-diet-by-type (content-provenance S3): channel-share concentration lens
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.1; backend verified py3.13; full suite green)
summary: Applies the SAME diet/concentration lens as the source-axis diet_self_audit producer to the CHANNEL axis (Source.source_type): reading_diet_by_type(session, *, days=30, top_n=1) reports each content channel's SHARE of the articles COLLECTED over the last N days, plus a concentration measure (dominant-channel top-share + the Gini coefficient) and an honest Wilson score 95% interval, reusing the shared src.signals.concentration (gini/top_share) + src.signals.intervals.wilson_interval machinery so the maths is byte-identical to the source-axis path. Distinct from /api/insights/source-types (raw channel counts / a filter facet) and from diet_self_audit (concentration across SOURCES): this is 'how much of my reading is newsletters vs web vs wiki'. HONESTY: counts + statistics only, NO score (a recursive key-walk test asserts no score/ranking key); NULL/blank source_type is bucketed 'untyped' identically to source_type_facets / the /api/articles filter, so a channel's diet share EQUALS what clicking that channel returns; honest empty state when the window holds no articles (note + caveat, never a fabricated split); a single-channel window reports 100% with Gini null + a VISIBLE small-n/single-channel caveat; the Wilson CI is disclosed as within-corpus (observational, self-selected sample), not the world. top_n defaults to 1 (a DELIBERATE, documented deviation from the source axis's 3): a channel axis has FEW actors, so the dominant-channel share is the interpretable concentration headline while the full per-channel breakdown + Gini carry the rest; the Wilson CI top_count is the EXACT integer sum of the top-k channel counts (never a float round), so top_share == top_count/total exactly. Window = Article.created_at (acquisition time, un-spoofable), served by idx_article_created_at; EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN confirms a range scan on idx_article_created_at + a PK source lookup that NEVER references the article content column, so the SQLCipher column-order codec never drags the 35 KB content payload (same claim the shipped source_type_facets makes, made precise). New GET /api/insights/reading-diet-by-type?days= (Depends(get_db); uncached direct call = no module-global TTL-cache cross-test pollution; days bounded 1-3650, out-of-range -> 422). NO migration (source_type + idx_source_type already exist). Tests in tests/test_wave6_reading_diet_type.py (9, ISOLATED in-memory StaticPool engines, NEVER SessionLocal; the endpoint test overrides get_db via app.dependency_overrides and pops it in finally): shares sum to 1.0 across channels; two sources same channel aggregate into one bucket; dominant-share + Gini + CI present with CI containing the point estimate and no score key; single-channel 100% + Gini null + small-n caveat; created_at window excludes old articles; NULL source_type -> untyped bucket (not folded into news); honest empty state; and parity with source_type_facets over a full window. VERIFIED py3.13 venv: FULL suite 2953 passed / 38 skipped / 0 failed (default order, no cross-test pollution), mypy 127 = baseline (unchanged), ruff --select=F,B clean, bandit -ll exit 0, single alembic head c1d2e3f4a5b6. Adversarially reviewed by parallel independent skeptic lenses (numerical correctness + honesty/no-score) -- both found no defect. LESSON: a channel-axis diet reuses the source-axis Gini/CI primitive verbatim, but top_n=1 (not 3) is the honest concentration headline for a few-actor axis, and the CI top-count must come from the exact INTEGER channel counts, not a round(top_share*total), so the reported share and its interval never disagree.

item: Article observed-date expression index (field-test 2026-07-08 Item 8 P0): index on coalesce(published_at, created_at) kills the 735s corpus-date-range full scan
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.1; migration + idempotent boot self-heal; full suite green)
summary: Adds an expression index ix_article_observed on articles(coalesce(published_at, created_at)) to kill the single biggest field-measured cost (Item 8 P0): the corpus date-range probe min(coalesce(published_at,created_at)), max(...) FROM articles measured 4,775 ms x 154 calls = 735 s -- a bare SCAN articles that dragged every ~35 KB article row (content column) through the SQLCipher codec because the coalesce expression had no index. Created by BOTH the alembic migration 5ea842778603 (down_revision c1d2e3f4a5b6 = the single head -> new single head) AND the idempotent PRAGMA/sqlite_master-checked boot self-heal maintenance.ensure_hot_indexes (HOT_INDEXES['ix_article_observed'], byte-identical DDL, matching the existing ix_mention_* pattern) so a DB predating the migration heals on boot. EMPIRICAL EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN (SQLite 3.45 stdlib + 3.51 SQLCipher, with and without ANALYZE): the min/max probe goes from bare 'SCAN articles' to 'SCAN articles USING [COVERING] INDEX ix_article_observed' (index-only), and the '>= cutoff' range filter to 'SEARCH articles USING [COVERING] INDEX ix_article_observed (<expr>>?)'. SQLite normalises the qualified query form coalesce(articles.published_at, articles.created_at) against the unqualified index expression, so no query-file edit is needed; all 7 cutoff sites (integrity/{collapse,actors,profile}, api/link_analysis, analytics/{copypasta,headline_body,recycled_claim}) already emit func.coalesce(Article.published_at, Article.created_at) (grep-verified) = the identical expression, so NO expression-alignment follow-up is needed. Per-site EXPLAIN coverage: the 735s min/max probe and the join/order-by-coalesce sites (link_analysis, profile novelty) USE the index; source-scoped profile uses the more-selective idx_article_source_id (never a full scan); the 5 ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT sites (collapse/actors/copypasta/headline_body/recycled_claim) keep a bounded rowid walk by the planner's cost model (LIMIT-bounded, not the unbounded 735s scan) -- a query-shape choice in their out-of-partition files, no index/expression defect. NOT declared on the ORM model: SQLAlchemy skips reflection of expression indexes (SAWarning), so alembic autogenerate/'alembic check' sees no model drift (verified 'No new upgrade operations detected', exit 0) -- the migration + self-heal are the two canonical creators, exactly like the mention indexes. Honesty: an index never changes an answer (zero-drift, test-pinned); no fabricated data. Tests in tests/test_wave7_date_index.py (9, isolated in-memory/tmp_path engines, never SessionLocal): self-heal creates exactly ['ix_article_observed'] + idempotent no-op + pre-existing no-op; alembic upgrade head creates it (subprocess); migration DDL == self-heal DDL lock-step + chained off the single head; the min/max probe is a bare scan WITHOUT the index and index-only WITH it (per the ledger USING-INDEX-is-healthy / bare-SCAN-is-the-smell classification, both SQLite wordings accepted); zero-drift min/max identical; the '>= cutoff' filter uses the index. VERIFIED py3.13 venv: FULL suite 2971 passed / 38 skipped / 0 failed (default order; baseline 2962 -> exactly +9 new, no cross-test pollution), mypy 127 = baseline (unchanged), ruff --select=F,B --extend-ignore=B008 clean, bandit -r src -ll exit 0, single alembic head 5ea842778603. LESSON: a covering EXPRESSION index turns min(coalesce)/max(coalesce) from a full heap SCAN (a SQLCipher decrypt per row) into an index-only scan; SQLite matches the index only on identical (normalised) expression text, and it silently SKIPS reflecting expression indexes so they never trip alembic's drift gate -- the migration + boot self-heal are the pair of creators, no model declaration needed.

item: Polled alert-strip memo cache (field test 2026-07-08 Item 8 P0 — /api/signals/alerts single-worker death-spiral)
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.1; backend verified py3.13; full suite green)
summary: The Home severity-alert strip polls GET /api/signals/alerts, whose compute_alerts re-ran a 45-day space-time find_convergences scan on EVERY poll (measured p50 23.7s / p95 60s over 156 calls on the live 59K-article corpus = the request-death-spiral driver; alerts.py itself had flagged this as 'a shared/memoised convergence pass is a later optimisation, not correctness'). NEW src/analytics/poll_cache.py memoises the compute_alerts result and refreshes it in the BACKGROUND, so the convergence scan never runs on the request/event-loop thread beyond the first cold call. Latency: p50/p95 ~24s/60s -> a memo dict read (sub-ms) on every warm poll; the ~24s convergence scan moves onto the background warm_cache thread (after each scrape pass + at boot) where it already runs for the space_time_convergence producer. HONEST BY CONSTRUCTION: the cached value is the SAME real compute_alerts payload (proven byte-identical to a fresh live compute in the test, minus the added freshness disclosure) — never fabricated or summarised; a visible as_of + cache_age_s + cached + cache_note are ADDED so the UI can disclose staleness (no hidden freshness); the method/caveat are unchanged ('a transparent rule over locally-cached signals, no network') — the memoisation adds an as_of, it does not change WHAT is computed; NO score anywhere (recursive key-walk test). SAFE BY CONSTRUCTION (mirrors rollup_serve): BIND-AWARE — each entry records session.get_bind() and is only served to a session bound to that SAME engine (a process-lifetime singleton must never answer for a DB it was not built from), so a cache built over one corpus never serves another; FALL BACK TO LIVE on any miss (cold cache, bind mismatch, error) — the first cold call computes live once + populates, every subsequent poll is a memo hit; the heavy compute never runs while the lock is held; the background self-heal runs on its own session_scope over the process store, guarded so at most one runs. Wired: signals_alerts routes through poll_cache.get_alerts; warm_cache gains a best-effort poll_cache.refresh(db) (non-blocking, own session_scope, mirrors rollup_serve.refresh/map_serve.refresh — runs regardless of the insights TTL, never delays the trending warm). OO_ALERTS_CACHE_TTL_S (default 600s) is the self-heal floor; serving continues (with an honest as_of) regardless of age so a stale-but-real value never triggers the request-thread recompute. ITEM 2 (trending-windows): VERIFIED — rollup_serve.serve_enabled() is ALREADY auto-on when duckdb is available (the 2026-07-02 field-ask flip, in the base f5c7ecd; serve_mode()='auto'), trending/trending_windows already wire rollup_serve.windowed_counts + a basis disclosure, and warm_cache already pre-warms the exact Home/Insights trending-windows keys — so no default flip was needed; the bind-gate + fallback-to-live are intact and NOW guarded by a Wave-1 regression test (a rollup built over corpus A falls back to the live query for a different-engine session B and returns B's own numbers, never A's rollup). tests/test_wave7_poll_cache.py (8, isolated per-test in-memory engines, NEVER SessionLocal; the endpoint test overrides get_db via app.dependency_overrides and pops it in finally): cached==fresh-live compute; a stale cache refreshed by the background path; a cold cache falls back to live; the payload carries as_of + no score key (recursive key-walk); poll_cache bind mismatch never serves another corpus; _same_bind identity gate; the endpoint served through the memo via dependency_overrides[get_db]; and the rollup_serve bind-mismatch Wave-1 regression (duckdb-gated). VERIFIED py3.13 venv: FULL suite green in default order (cross-pollution check), mypy 127 = baseline (0 new errors), ruff --select=F,B --extend-ignore=B008 clean, bandit -ll exit 0, single alembic head 5ea842778603 after rebase onto Session P's date-index merge (no migration added — Session P owns the head). LESSON: a polled endpoint whose handler runs a multi-second scan is a single-worker death-spiral; the fix is a BACKGROUND-refreshed memo that serves stale-but-real with a visible as_of and falls back to live, and it MUST be bind-aware (a process-lifetime singleton, like rollup_serve, must never answer for a DB it was not built from) — reuse the rollup_serve _same_bind identity gate rather than inventing a new one.

item: Persian (Solar-Hijri/Jalali) + Hungarian date recall (field-test 2026-07-08 Item 8 P2 / F4): the two worst per-language date gaps (fa 0%, hu 22%)
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.1; verified py3.13; FULL suite 2999 passed / 38 skipped / 0 failed; mypy 127; ruff F,B; bandit -ll exit 0; no migration)
summary: Fixes the two worst per-language date-recall gaps the field test flagged: Persian (fa) sat at 0% (Iranian datelines use the Solar-Hijri/Jalali calendar with Persian month NAMES, long OMITTED because a blind month-number mapping would fabricate) and Hungarian at 22%. PERSIAN (fa-GATED): Solar-Hijri dates are now CONVERTED to Gregorian by EXACT calendar arithmetic (the same honesty class as the shipped era-name 令和6年 and Buddhist-Era conversions -- a conversion, never an inference), so the map places them at the correct moment instead of missing them. The conversion is the public-domain jalaali-js algorithm reimplemented in pure Python (ZERO new dependency): _jdiv (truncate-toward-zero, needed for the g2d div(gm-8,6) term where Python floor // would differ) + _jal_cal (33-year break table) + _greg_to_jdn/_jdn_to_greg + _jalali_month_length + _jalali_valid. VERIFIED exact against the independent jdatetime library over 54,707 days (0 mismatches), a full round-trip over Jalali years 1200-1500 (0 failures), Julian-Day monotonicity (0 gaps), and the documented anchors (1 Farvardin 1403 = 2024-03-20 Nowruz [the task golden]; 22 Bahman 1357 = 1979-02-11 the Revolution; 30 Esfand 1403 = 2025-03-20 leap-year boundary). _jalali_valid REJECTS an impossible Jalali date (day past the month length, incl. Esfand 30 in a non-leap year) BEFORE converting -- a rollover would invent a neighbouring day -- and range-checks the Gregorian result through _valid (skip-never-guess). _FA_MONTHS lists the 12 names + Arabic-yeh variants (فروردين/ارديبهشت/تير/شهريور/دي) so a date in either script resolves without a normalisation pass + امرداد. Three fa-gated loops run FIRST (before the ISO/numeric loops): _FA_DMY_RE (day+month+year -> day), _FA_NUM_YMD_RE (numeric ۱۴۰۳/۰۳/۱۱ -> Jalali ONLY when the year is in the Solar-Hijri news range 1200..1450, else falls through to the Gregorian numeric/ISO paths), _FA_MY_RE (month+year -> month precision). This ALSO FIXES A LIVE FABRICATION: a numeric Jalali '۱۴۰۳/۰۳/۱۱' used to be stored as 1403-03-11 CE (a medieval date -- 1403 passes the CE window); it now converts correctly to 2024-05-31. fa-GATED because several Jalali names are ordinary Persian words (تیر arrow, مهر affection/seal, دی, بهمن avalanche, اسفند the plant): the gate + the mandatory adjacent day/year keep prose from inventing a date, and a Jalali name in a non-fa article is never read. Persian 'May' (مه/می) stays deliberately withheld. HUNGARIAN: (a) hu-gated year-first NAMED month-only '2024. június' -> June 2024 month precision (its mandatory trailing period after the YEAR distinguishes it from an English sentence boundary '...in 2024. March was cold', applied ONLY under the hu hint; the pattern also correctly resolves Hungarian's common SUFFIXED month forms '2024. júniusban/júniusi' -> the month); (b) hu-gated spaced year-first numeric '2024. 06. 11.' (the official Hungarian spelling; kept hu-GATED rather than folded into the shared numeric regex because the spaced form in other languages is more often a hierarchical list/version item -- 'Version 2024. 12. 31.', 'item 2024. 1. 5.' -- than a date, so widening every language would invent dates). The already-working day form + full Jan-Dec vocab were confirmed unchanged. LOCKSTEP: datediag gains two fa-GATED Persian month probes (reusing the extractor's OWN _FA_DMY_RE/_FA_MY_RE = exact lockstep) so a Jalali date is date-like ONLY in fa; analyze_article reports zero actionable gap on fa Jalali named+numeric dates, and the SAME Persian text in a non-fa article yields no phantom gap (gated token outside its language = no gap); MONTH_VOCAB_LANGS comment updated. COVERAGE (representative Iranian/Hungarian press datelines): fa 30%->80% (and the base 30% included 2 numeric-Jalali FABRICATIONS now corrected -- the real corpus was 0% because named Jalali forms were omitted entirely), hu 40%->80%. STALE-TEST UPDATE (disjoint from the parallel session's queries.py/insights.py): tests/test_dateextract_batch_a_languages.py::test_persian_solar_hijri_names_are_not_fabricated encoded the OLD deliberate-omission (asserted Solar-Hijri names extract []); the task explicitly mandates reversing that omission via exact conversion, so it was reframed to test_persian_solar_hijri_names_convert_not_fabricate -- asserting the CORRECT converted date, that it is NOT the blind month-number mapping (1403-01-15), and that a Jalali name outside fa still yields nothing (the anti-fabrication intent preserved). ADVERSARIALLY VERIFIED (5 distinct lenses run directly in the venv): no fabrication (homographs in prose, absurd years, invalid Esfand days all skip or fall back to honest month precision), conversions exact, gates hold, no cross-language regression after gating, no catastrophic backtracking (new regexes sub-millisecond on backtracking bait; scaling linear-ish, the slight super-linearity is the pre-existing O(matches^2) claim() logic material only for unrealistic all-dates inputs). tests/test_wave8_dates_fa_hu.py (28): golden Jalali conversions + leap-year Esfand validity, named/numeric fa datelines, the numeric-Jalali fabrication fix, Gregorian-with-Persian-digits still resolving, Arabic-yeh spelling, the fa gate (Jalali outside fa skipped) + homograph safety, May-withheld, Hungarian day/month-only/spaced-numeric + the hu gate (EN sentence boundary + other languages don't fabricate; ordinal-year not a date), and the datediag lockstep (zero actionable gap in fa, no phantom gap outside fa). LESSON: to HONESTLY extract a Solar-Hijri (or any non-Gregorian) calendar date you must CONVERT it (verified exact against an independent impl over decades + round-trip + monotonic), never omit it AND never blind-map the month number; the jalaali-js div() truncates toward zero (Python floor // differs on the g2d term); a language-specific numeric/format widening (spaced year-first) belongs GATED to that language, not folded into the shared regex, because the same shape is a list/version item elsewhere (miss over invent).

item: 2-hop keyword graph bounding + deadline (field-test 2026-07-08 Item 8): GET /api/insights/graph never 60s->503
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.1; backend verified py3.13; full suite green; adversarially reviewed)
summary: Root cause (verified): GET /api/insights/graph?level=keyword&term=...&hops=2 measured 60s -> HTTP 503 -> a broken frontend at 974K keywords. insights_graph -> queries.layered_graph, which for the keyword level called associations() for hop-1 then looped the top hop-2 relatives calling associations() AGAIN (~6 calls); associations() is batch-optimized (n_b via the maintained article_count counter, one Keyword IN, ZERO per-co-keyword COUNT(DISTINCT)/session.get -- re-verified it still holds), but each call recomputed the corpus-total COUNT(DISTINCT article_id) scan AND scanned the FULL article set of every seed/relative, so the fan-out compounded past the 60s statement deadline (which mapped to a 503). FIX (queries.py): (1) layered_graph computes the PMI corpus-total denominator ONCE and threads it into every associations() call via a new corpus_total kwarg (was recomputed per call; byte-identical PMI); (2) each term's article set is sampled to article_cap via a DETERMINISTIC, time-spanning index projection ordered[(i*N)//cap] (spans the full sorted id range -- keeps the oldest, only mildly under-represents the newest ~1/cap, so it does NOT bias toward recent data = cross-time-recall sacred; reproducible; bounds both the co-occurrence aggregation and the SQL IN() size); (3) the hop-2 fan-out is capped (GRAPH_HOP2_PARENTS=4, was hardcoded 5), total nodes capped in BOTH hop-1 and hop-2 loops (limit_nodes=36), edges capped (GRAPH_MAX_EDGES=160, keeps the strongest by co-occurrence weight); (4) an optional soft wall-clock time_budget_s (endpoint passes _graph_budget_s(), default 15s < the 60s hard deadline) stops expanding to hop-2 and returns the hop-1 partial. EXACT BOUNDS: <=1 corpus-total scan (was ~6), <=1+GRAPH_HOP2_PARENTS(4)=5 associations() calls, <=GRAPH_ARTICLE_CAP(900) articles sampled per term, <=limit_nodes(36) nodes, <=GRAPH_MAX_EDGES(160) edges. GRAPH_ARTICLE_CAP is 900 (NOT 1200) so the sampled set fed to co_rows' article_id.in_(...) stays UNDER SQLite's 999 bound-variable cap -- the repo-wide invariant (cf. latest.py:_SQL_IN_CHUNK=900); a scaling-skeptic caught 1200 would risk 'too many SQL variables' (OperationalError -> 500, NOT caught by on_timeout) on a 999-limit build for exactly the high-frequency seeds this targets. HONESTY (all preserved): every cap that truncates sets out['bounded']=True and appends a VISIBLE disclosure to out['caveat'] (the field all three frontend mind-map consumers already render), never a silent cut; associations surfaces articles_sampled/articles_bounded and keeps n_articles_with_term = the TRUE population (not the sample size); NO composite score key added (bounded/disclosure/degraded/articles_sampled/articles_bounded; pmi/weight/size/cooccur remain named stats -- a recursive key-walk test asserts no score/ranking/rank key). DEGRADE-NEVER-503 (insights.py): _deadlined gains an on_timeout hook; the /graph endpoint (both the term path and the article_ids radial-map path) passes it so a hard StatementTimeout returns an honest actionable 200 payload (_graph_degraded) instead of a 503; the degraded payload carries NO truthy 'locked' field (which would bounce the SPA to /unlock), and MIRRORS its level -- level='article'+n_articles for the article path, the co-occurrence method+term for keyword/family/supergroup -- so a client branching on level isn't misled on the timeout path (correctness-skeptic finding). BYTE-IDENTITY: corpus_total/article_cap both default None => the /associations endpoint, keyword_stats and every existing caller are byte-unchanged (test_readmodel_seam whole-dict equality + test_associations_perf PMI recomputation both still green). PERF (local micro-benchmark, 3000-article synthetic corpus, hub in all 3000): single associations 36ms->17ms; full 2-hop graph 131ms->76ms -- both wins (total-once + sampling) measurable even at small scale and compounding at 974K keywords where the before-path did ~6 full-corpus COUNT-DISTINCT scans + unbounded per-term co-occurrence. Tests in tests/test_wave8_graph_deadline.py (12, ISOLATED in-memory StaticPool engines, NEVER SessionLocal; endpoint tests override get_db via app.dependency_overrides and pop it in finally, module-global TTL cache disabled to avoid cross-test pollution): node cap enforced+disclosed; edge cap enforced (kept edges are the strongest, sorted desc)+disclosed; hop-2 fan-out capped on a dense synthetic graph; soft budget (time_budget_s=0.0) returns a hop-1 partial with no hop-2 nodes; article sample bounded+deterministic+discloses the true count; corpus_total threaded is byte-identical; a normal small graph is NOT flagged bounded (no spurious disclosure); no-cap omits the bounded fields; the endpoint returns a real bounded graph 200 (never 503) with a visible disclosure and no score key; a hard StatementTimeout degrades to a 200 message (not 503); the article_ids path degrades as level='article'; level/term validation unchanged. VERIFIED py3.13 venv: FULL suite 3031 passed / 7 skipped / 0 failed (default order, no cross-test pollution), mypy 127 = baseline (unchanged), ruff --select=F,B --extend-ignore=B008 clean, bandit -r src -ll exit 0, single alembic head 5ea842778603 (no migration). Adversarially reviewed by 4 independent skeptic lenses (correctness/honesty/scaling/test-rigor): the scaling lens caught the 1200>999 IN()-cap defect (fixed to 900) and the correctness lens caught the article_ids degraded mislabel (fixed to level='article'); both fixes landed with pinning tests. KNOWN RESIDUALS (pre-existing, out of this item's scope, bounded by the deadline->degraded-200 net, noted honestly): resolve_keyword's LIKE '%norm%' fallback (fired at most once on hop-1 when the seed has no exact normalized match) is the one remaining query whose cost grows with keyword count; the co-keyword 'kids' IN() in associations is un-chunked (pre-existing; the deployed pysqlcipher build clearly carries the modern 32766 variable limit, else /associations would already break on common terms). LESSON: a background review agent that experiments by editing source then `git checkout`/stash-restoring can silently revert your UNCOMMITTED changes; a subsequent `git add <file>` then stages nothing and the commit drops that file -- 'sizes lie, diffs don't': after committing, verify `git show HEAD --stat` lists EVERY file you changed (a '2 files changed' when you touched 3 is the tell), and fold each file into HEAD before spawning agents that mutate the working tree.

item: fa Jalali fabrication fix-forward (post-merge audit of PR #590): 3 wrong-date repros killed
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.1; 151 date tests + 5 new regressions green; mypy 127; ruff F,B; bandit exit 0; no migration)
summary: A post-merge adversarial audit of #590 found the Jalali->Gregorian ARITHMETIC exact but 3 concrete inputs STORING a wrong date: (V1, new in #590) _FA_MY_RE had no left boundary, and دی (Dey) is the word-TAIL of common Persian words (عادی ordinary / اقتصادی economic), so a bare Jalali year after such a word fabricated a month-precision date ('سال عادی ۱۴۰۳' stored Dey 1403 = 2024-12-21); (V2) the fa numeric router claimed the span only on SUCCESS, so an invalid (۱۴۰۲/۱۲/۳۰ = 30 Esfand in a non-leap year) or out-of-window (۱۴۲۰/۰۱/۰۱) Jalali date FELL THROUGH to the generic numeric loop and was stored as a medieval CE date (1402-12-30 / 1420-01-01 pass _valid since _MIN_YEAR=1000); (V3) a day-first numeric with a Jalali-range year (۱۱/۰۳/۱۴۰۳) was read by the generic DMY loop as 1403-03-11 CE. FIXES (all surgical): a (?<![\w‌]) lookbehind on _FA_MY_RE (blocks a word char AND the Persian ZWNJ compound joiner; legitimate month usage is space/punct-preceded — 'در دی ۱۴۰۳' still extracts); CLAIM-ON-ROUTE in the fa numeric router (once the year says Jalali the span is Persian-calendar territory: an invalid/out-of-window date is claimed + SKIPPED, never guessed, and the claim stops the generic loops re-reading the same digits as CE); a fa-gated guard claiming day-first numerics with a Jalali-range year (field order is an assumption we refuse — skipped, never converted, never CE). datediag stays in LOCKSTEP by construction (it imports the same compiled _FA_MY_RE object). 5 pinning regression tests + hand-verified controls (valid YMD/named/leap/Gregorian-in-fa all unchanged). LESSON (refines VERIFY-BEFORE-PUSH): #590's own pre-push verification claimed 5 skeptic lenses yet all 3 fabrications survived — because the lenses verified the POSITIVE space (goldens convert exactly) and never attacked the NEGATIVE space (inputs that must yield NOTHING: word-tail homographs, invalid dates falling through a claim-on-success router, order-ambiguous forms). A no-fabrication skeptic must generate SHOULD-BE-EMPTY inputs per pattern (every regex alternation tail, every router failure path) and assert []. Corollary: a language-router must CLAIM-ON-ROUTE (consume the span even when validation fails) or the generic loops re-read the digits under another calendar.

item: Session forensics + data-dir inventory (automate the maintainer's root-cause asks)
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.1; 9 new tests green; mypy 127; ruff F,B; bandit exit 0; no migration; full-suite confirmation running at push)
summary: Born from the 2026-07-09 field event, where root-causing the OOM crash / 981s unlock / 130GB-vs-11.7GB mystery needed THREE manual terminal commands from the maintainer. The app now answers them ITSELF in every diagnostics export (a new session-forensics.json member of the /all zip + a session_forensics block in the debug bundle + GET /api/diagnostics/session-forensics): (1) DATA-DIR INVENTORY — per-entry recursive sizes with the DB/-wal/-shm called out and ORPHANED BACKUP/RESTORE STAGING detected by the exact name patterns the backup code uses (.bak-build-*/.restore-*/*.oopart, grounded in artifact.py:418/:515 + folder_backup.py:48 — never guessed), with a loud plaintext_snapshot flag when a crashed backup left a decrypted corpus.db on disk (the prime 120GB suspect AND an at-rest-encryption hazard); symlinks never followed, contents never read, sizes+app-owned names only. (2) CLEAN-SHUTDOWN SENTINEL — session_state.json stamped running at boot (lifespan pre-yield) and clean at shutdown (post-yield); the next boot reports the previous session's verdict, an unclean end paired with the collector's last collect_perf RSS sample and worded as CONSISTENT-WITH-OOM — explicitly an INFERENCE, never a kernel-log fact (no fabrication). (3) UNLOCK TIMING — the unlock path stats the -wal BEFORE the first connection (a large WAL predicts recovery time inside init_db) and times its synchronous phases (init_db incl. migrations/WAL-recovery vs airplane guard), persisting the record so it survives into the NEXT boot's export. Everything best-effort (a forensics failure never touches boot/unlock — try/except at every seam); local-only, nothing transmitted. tests/test_session_forensics.py (9, isolated OO_DATA_DIR=tmp_path, never SessionLocal): inventory sizes + staging/plaintext detection, symlink-out-of-tree never counted (negative space), empty/recreated dir never raises, unclean-end verdict + RSS sample + inference wording, clean end carries no OOM block, first-boot=unknown-not-a-guess, unlock record round-trip + survives next boot, wal_bytes honest-None, source-level wiring guard (endpoint + bundle + /all member + lifespan + unlock hooks).

item: A1: bounded-concurrency cap + single-flight + deadlines on the heavy read endpoints (field-test 2026-07-08 Item 8 P0 death-spiral)
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.1; backend verified py3.13; FULL suite 3053 passed / 39 skipped / 0 failed; mypy 127=baseline; ruff F,B clean; bandit -ll 0; single alembic head 5ea842778603; 5 adversarial skeptic lenses run pre-push)
summary: The measured death spiral (2026-07-08 3rd batch): Home polls fire every few seconds, each heavy analytics query takes 60-200s on the 60K-article encrypted corpus, and nothing is cancelled when the client gives up -- so new polls pile onto unfinished ones (in_flight seen 9-10), all thrashing the ONE SQLCipher connection until the single worker freezes for minutes; caching/indexing alone can't stop the STACKING. NEW src/api/heavy.py is a request-admission guard: (a) a GLOBAL concurrency cap (OO_HEAVY_CONCURRENCY default 4) -- the (N+1)th distinct heavy compute fast-fails with a typed HeavyBusy -> honest 429 'busy, retry' after a short acquire wait, so in_flight stays bounded and no 200s compute holds a threadpool token; (b) SINGLE-FLIGHT -- identical concurrent requests share ONE compute (bind-qualified keys via flight_key so a flight over DB-A is never shared with a request on DB-B), a slow leader makes a follower degrade honestly (429) never recompute. Both busy/timeout paths RAISE so a degraded/partial value is NEVER cached. Wired: insights._deadlined now runs its compute under run_heavy (associations/graph/keyword-stats inherit cap+single-flight), and the cached-but-un-deadlined heavy endpoints (top/trending/trending-windows/latest/corpus-www/sentiment/sources/keywords/coordination/server-locations) moved onto _deadlined so a deadline+cap is added on the cold-recompute path (hot cache hits unchanged); the fully-unprotected scans get guarded_read (cap+single-flight+statement deadline): insights/lunar-correlation and signals/flood/bury/disputed-chronology/story-propagation/supply-chain-ripple. HONESTY: the value returned is the leader's REAL computed value (single-flight collapses DUPLICATE work, never fabricates); no score. tests/test_heavy_guard.py (9) attacks the NEGATIVE space: the excess is REFUSED not run (cap=1), a duplicate never triggers a second compute, a slow leader degrades not recomputes, busy->429 / deadline->503, bind-qualified keys, no spurious 429 on a single request. ADVERSARIALLY VERIFIED (skeptic, running repros): all 7 vectors REFUTED -- semaphore released on every path incl BaseException (no cap leak over 200 failing computes), cap-bail follower gets HeavyBusy never a stale None (fl.busy set before the event, happens-before via the Event lock), _deadlined never caches a busy/timeout/on_timeout payload, flight is bind-qualified, no lock-ordering deadlock (_LOCK never held across sem.acquire). Note (pre-existing, not this change): the insights _read_cache TTL is bare-key/bind-unaware -- documented, and safe because busy/timeout always RAISE before the store. LESSON: a polled endpoint whose handler runs a multi-second scan is only half-fixed by caching -- the request STACKING must be stopped structurally with a global concurrency cap (serialising is faster than thrashing the one connection) + server-side single-flight; make busy/timeout RAISE so a degraded value is never cached, and bind-qualify the single-flight key so a process-lifetime guard never shares across databases.

item: A2: job-ify the heavy synchronous handlers -- governments/load-standard, enrich-source-types, keyword-tags/backfill (field-test 2026-07-08 Item 4 + Item 8 P1)
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.1; backend verified py3.13; FULL suite 3053 passed / 39 skipped / 0 failed; mypy 127=baseline; ruff F,B clean; bandit -ll 0; single alembic head 5ea842778603; 5 adversarial skeptic lenses run pre-push)
key_paths: src/jobs/background.py src/jobs/__init__.py src/api/governments.py src/api/diagnostics.py src/api/insights.py src/api/jobs.py tests/test_background_job.py tests/test_a2_job_endpoints.py
summary: governments/load-standard (2.9 min), diagnostics/enrich-source-types (8.5 min) and insights/keyword-tags/backfill (1 min) ran their whole body SYNCHRONOUSLY in the request handler -- a threadpool token held for minutes and (for the DB writers) the single-writer gate held across the whole run, so the button froze the app and blocked collection (the FastAPI single-worker freeze). NEW src/jobs/background.py: a generic run-to-completion, cancellable, task-manager-visible background job (worker thread + cooperative stop Event + live progress + a registry); NOT persisted/resumable (these are BOUNDED one-shots, a crash just means re-run). Converted: governments -> a job with per-indicator progress + cancel that COMMITS PER INDICATOR so the writer gate is RELEASED between indicators (collection interleaves) instead of held for the whole run -- a genuine fix over the old one-transaction handler (SKEPTIC-CONFIRMED via repro: db.commit() on a session_scope session fires after_transaction_end and releases the gate; a competing writer proceeds immediately after each commit); the tab reads figures as they land. enrich + backfill -> background jobs (OPAQUE to progress, they wrap library calls that loop internally). Each endpoint returns immediately ({started, job}); a /status endpoint + /api/jobs surface progress; the network jobs still refuse up front under airplane mode (409); the three DB writers join the /api/jobs writer-arbitration set (_DB_WRITER_KINDS). HONESTY (skeptic D1/D2/D3, fixed in the same session before push): a `cancellable` flag -- only governments (which checks ctx.stopping) advertises the Cancel button; the opaque jobs offer NO cancel action (offering a no-op button would be theatre) and a finished opaque worker is NEVER mislabelled 'cancelled'; cancel_job returns an honest 'cannot be interrupted mid-pass' for the opaque ones; governments returns an honest `complete` flag (False when it stopped early on cancel/airplane). store_figures is idempotent per vintage so a partial run + re-run never double-counts. tests/test_background_job.py (7: done/progress/cooperative-cancel/non-cancellable-never-cancelled/no-double-start[64-thread barrier repro: exactly 1 worker]/crash-captured/registry) + tests/test_a2_job_endpoints.py (each endpoint starts a job, status endpoints, /api/jobs surfacing + arbitration + cancel routing, airplane 409 preserved). FRONTEND FOLLOW-UP (BETA, noted in the PR): poll the new /status endpoints or /api/jobs, show progress, and per Item 4 drop the Governments 'Load' button for auto-load; the response contract is now {started, job} (a dict -- the current button handlers won't error, just show a different toast until wired). LESSON: a def handler runs in the threadpool but a DB writer still holds the single-writer gate across its whole body -- move it to a worker thread AND commit per unit so the gate releases between units; and NEVER advertise a Cancel affordance a worker can't honour (gate the action + the 'cancelled' state on whether the worker actually checks ctx.stopping).

item: A3: /status served through a data-aware cache; the mention count stays EXACT for honesty (field-test 2026-07-08 Item 8 P1)
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.1; backend verified py3.13; FULL suite 3053 passed / 39 skipped / 0 failed; mypy 127=baseline; ruff F,B clean; bandit -ll 0; single alembic head 5ea842778603; 5 adversarial skeptic lenses run pre-push)
summary: GET /api/insights/status ran FIVE uncached full-table counts on every poll, and count(*) FROM keyword_mentions was the single slowest query in the field diagnostics: 724 ms x 172 polls = 124 s (a full scan of the multi-GB mention table dragged through the SQLCipher codec). FIX: the /status endpoint is served through a DATA-AWARE cache key (bind + PRAGMA data_version + total_changes()) so a burst of identical polls hits the cache (collapsing the 172 repeated scans -- the actual field cost) while any write invalidates it (progress stays live) and a different DB never collides (test-safe). HONESTY CALL (skeptic D1, CONFIRMED then addressed pre-push): an earlier cut served `mentions` from the maintained counter SUM(Keyword.article_count) (== count(*) mentions under the unique (keyword_id,article_id) index) with a counter_envelope basis -- but the envelope's basis is keyed on the reconcile WATERMARK, not actual correctness, so a cascade delete (Article delete -> ON DELETE CASCADE bypasses the per-article counter maintenance) drifts the counter while basis still says 'exact', presenting a WRONG number as exact = a breach of the honesty non-negotiable. Reverted to the real, always-exact count(*) and dropped the counters/mentions_basis fields; the cache (which is what actually removes the 124 s) stays. A correctness-gated counter-serve (basis tied to the corpus epoch + a cascade-delete hook, not the watermark) is noted as a possible future optimisation. tests/test_status_counters.py: mentions == the exact row count; a DRIFT GUARD (a raw KeywordMention makes SUM(article_count) understate the truth while status stays exact -- the reason we don't serve the sum); the data-aware key is stable / write-invalidated / bind-distinct; response shape is purely additive (no key removed). SKEPTIC REFUTED: counter_envelope is O(keywords) not a mention scan; the data_version+total_changes probe catches both same-connection and cross-connection committed writes (no stale-through-a-write hit). LESSON: a maintained-counter derivation is only honest to serve as 'exact' if its basis reflects ACTUAL correctness -- a reconcile-watermark proxy misses cascade-delete drift; when the real win is collapsing REPEATED scans, a data-aware cache over the EXACT value beats trading the exact value for a possibly-drifting counter.

item: A4: correct the OECD share-price FRED ids to the 2-letter ISO code (field-test 2026-07-08 Item 1)
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.1; backend verified py3.13; FULL suite 3053 passed / 39 skipped / 0 failed; mypy 127=baseline; ruff F,B clean; bandit -ll 0; single alembic head 5ea842778603; 5 adversarial skeptic lenses run pre-push)
summary: Every OECD MEI share-price entry in configs/index_feeds.yml embedded a 3-letter ISO country code (SPASTT01DEUM661N) but FRED's SPASTT01<CC>M661N family uses the 2-letter code (SPASTT01DEM661N). The wrong ids 404'd on FRED, so every OECD index stored no data and its whole continent rendered empty on the Indices board (Africa/S.America/Oceania/Europe empty, Asia only Nikkei, N.America only US). Fixed symbol + url for all 19 (US/CN/ES/KR live-confirmed; the rest follow the same documented FRED/OECD 2-letter pattern, not all live-verifiable offline -- a wrong id still fails LOUDLY as a dead series, never fabricates). Added an offline format guard (tests/test_index_feeds_fred_format.py): every OECD id must match ^SPASTT01[A-Z]{2}M6[0-9]{2}N$ + a dedicated no-stale-3-letter regression assertion + symbol-embedded-in-url. ADVERSARIALLY VERIFIED (skeptic): all 19 ISO-3->ISO-2 mappings correct incl. the traps SWE->SE (not SW), CHE->CH, CHL->CL, KOR->KR, ZAF->ZA, MEX->MX. LESSON: an offline format guard catches a wrong-SHAPE id (3-letter) but not a mis-MAPPED-but-valid 2-letter code -- hand-verify each mapping; a wrong id still degrades loudly (dead series) rather than fabricating.

item: A5: filter multi-word stopword PHRASES in n-gram extraction -- activates the inert vendored entries (field-test 2026-07-08 KW / vi leak)
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.1; backend verified py3.13; FULL suite 3053 passed / 39 skipped / 0 failed; mypy 127=baseline; ruff F,B clean; bandit -ll 0; single alembic head 5ea842778603; 5 adversarial skeptic lenses run pre-push)
summary: Investigating the Vietnamese stoplist leak (12K+ vi junk keywords) confirmed vi.txt IS loaded and consulted at extraction (no wiring bypass) -- but the n-gram path only dropped a phrase when a COMPONENT word was a stopword, so the 379 of 645 vi.txt entries that are MULTI-WORD phrases (e.g. 'bao gio' = when, whose syllables 'bao'/'gio' are not standalone entries) leaked as keywords. The extractor now also drops an n-gram whose JOINED form is a stopword entry, activating those existing vendored entries across every scoped list -- no new/fabricated stopwords. HONEST SCOPE: a PARTIAL fix for syllable-segmented languages (vi/zh/ja/th) -- the single-syllable fragments + multi-syllable function words that fragment across token boundaries still leak; the real cure is a word segmenter (ledger P4.4, ruling-gated). ADVERSARIALLY VERIFIED (skeptic, running repros): NO over-filtering of content -- global_stopwords() has ZERO multi-word entries so en/fr extraction is BYTE-UNCHANGED (they use the language_stopwords branch, not scoped); only ca/es/fa/ru/ur/vi scoped lists have any multi-word entries and every one is a function/particle/idiom phrase (most are redundant -- a component is already a single stopword -- so the new line adds behaviour only for the vi non-redundant set, all 145 of which are function words/particles/reduplicatives/idioms with ZERO content-noun collisions tested against viet nam/ha noi/chinh phu/kinh te/khi hau...). tests/test_multiword_stopword_ngram.py: the mechanism (a joined stopword bigram dropped, an adjacent content bigram + the individual tokens kept), the real vi 'bao gio' end-to-end, an English over-filter guard. LESSON: the vendored stopwords-iso lists carry MULTI-WORD phrases that a per-token n-gram check leaves inert; matching the joined n-gram against the set activates them for free and is safe because the language-agnostic global stoplist has no multi-word entries (so en/fr are byte-unchanged) and each scoped list's multi-word entries are curated function phrases, not content.

item: A6: poll_cache serve isolation (deep-copy) + per-level degraded-graph wording + layered_graph window alignment (the #591 nits)
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.1; backend verified py3.13; FULL suite 3053 passed / 39 skipped / 0 failed; mypy 127=baseline; ruff F,B clean; bandit -ll 0; single alembic head 5ea842778603; 5 adversarial skeptic lenses run pre-push)
summary: Three small correctness/honesty riders. (a) poll_cache._decorate did a SHALLOW top-level merge ({**payload, ...}), so a caller that mutated a NESTED object in a served alert payload would corrupt the cached object that later serves + followers reuse; now returns a copy.deepcopy so each serve is independent (the alert payload is a small bounded set of convergence clusters, so the copy is negligible next to the convergence scan the module exists to avoid). SKEPTIC-VERIFIED deepcopy-safe: compute_alerts returns only plain primitives/dicts/lists (article_id sets are list-ified before return, dates are ISO strings), so no non-copyable object reaches _decorate. (b) _graph_degraded (the hard-deadline net) reused the keyword PMI method/caveat for family/supergroup timeouts; now mirrors the REAL per-level wording (families -> shared-article overlap; super-groups -> curated groups) while keeping the actionable 'narrow the term' message; the article path still reports level='article'+n_articles. (c) layered_graph passed start (derived from days) but not days to its inner associations() calls, so the disclosed inner window.days read None; now threads days=days -- PROVEN byte-identical for the graph output (layered_graph pre-sets start so associations' 'if days and not start' guard is False; the window sub-dict isn't surfaced into the graph). tests/test_a6_riders.py: serve-isolation (mutating a served result leaves the next serve + the source payload intact), per-level degraded wording, no-score. LESSON: {**dict} only copies the top level -- a background-refreshed cache that returns nested structures must deep-copy per serve or a downstream mutation silently corrupts every future serve; a degraded/fallback payload should mirror the REAL per-branch method so a client that switches on level isn't misled on the failure path.

item: E1: honest 429 backpressure retry in api() + single-flight Home pollers
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.1; frontend browser-unverified; node --check; i18n --min 100 green; guard test)

Taught the shared api() helper an honest 'busy -- retrying' path: bounded retries (max 4) honouring Retry-After with one throttled non-blocking toast, so backpressure reads as the app protecting itself, not breaking. item: D0: /status cache key probes data_version on a PINNED connection (fix-forward of #595/A3)
status: shipped (draft PR #600 onto 0.1; backend verified py3.13; FULL suite green per item [D0 3144, D1 3149, D2 3153, D3+D4 3165, D5 3167 passed / 7 skipped / 0 failed]; mypy 127=baseline; ruff F,B + advisory clean; bandit -ll 0; single alembic head 5ea842778603 untouched; pre-push two-connection negative-space skeptics + an adversarial review agent)
summary: The /status data-aware cache key probed PRAGMA data_version + total_changes() on the REQUEST connection. Under the churning overflow pool (session.py closes overflow connections on return), a poll after another connection's commit ran on a FRESH connection where total_changes()==0 and data_version is connection-local + unchanged, so the key never moved and the stale count was served for the whole 120s TTL. REPRODUCED deterministically under NullPool (the production fresh-connection shape): the old probe returns the SAME key across a cross-connection write while data_version stays 2 as the corpus grows. FIX: read data_version on our OWN pinned, probe-only connection per engine -- never a writer's, so it observes every other-connection commit (the pragma's documented purpose) and is stable otherwise; read-only, lock-guarded, rebuilt once on error (dispose-safe); a strong engine ref pins id(engine) against recycle onto a stale probe (the fixture hazard), cleared between tests by _reset_status_probe_for_tests. ALSO: heavy.py flight_key's unknown-bind fallback now mints a per-CALLER uuid key instead of the constant that let two unknown-bind callers over different DBs wrongly SHARE a flight result (the comment claimed per-caller; the code shared). Corrected the now-precise cache-invalidation docstrings in insights.py + queries.py. NEGATIVE-SPACE TEST: two-connection invalidation under NullPool (writer commits on a different connection than the poller) + an end-to-end endpoint test; autouse fixture clears the read-cache + probe registry so a recycled fixture-engine id() can never flake. LESSON: PRAGMA data_version is connection-LOCAL and BLIND on a fresh connection -- a cross-connection change probe must read it on a long-lived pinned connection; total_changes() carryover via pool connection-reuse can MASK the bug in a default QueuePool test, so the skeptic must use NullPool (the production overflow-closing shape) to reproduce it.

item: D1: bound /api/insights/latest near-dup by folding a content prefix into the candidate scan (P1.4, 59s)
status: shipped (draft PR #600 onto 0.1; backend verified py3.13; FULL suite green per item [D0 3144, D1 3149, D2 3153, D3+D4 3165, D5 3167 passed / 7 skipped / 0 failed]; mypy 127=baseline; ruff F,B + advisory clean; bandit -ll 0; single alembic head 5ea842778603 untouched; pre-push two-connection negative-space skeptics + an adversarial review agent)
summary: The Home 'Latest' near-dup collapse read the FULL Article object (whole content blob + every trailing column) for up to near_dup_cap=300 candidates through the SQLCipher codec -- a SECOND decrypt of pages the candidate scan already decrypted to reach word_count. MEASURED on an encrypted store (600 x 40KB bodies): a word_count scan (47ms) + a full-content read (45ms) = ~92ms/300 rows of codec work, plus MinHash over 300 x 40KB docs. CRITICAL FINDING: substr() does NOT short-circuit the overflow decrypt on SQLCipher (verified: substr(content,1,4000) costs the same 45ms as full content -- SQLite reads the whole value to apply substr), so a prefix query alone saves nothing on decrypt. The real win is to read the bounded prefix in the SAME candidate scan that already pays the decrypt for word_count. FIX: fold func.substr(content,1,prefix) into the candidate query (index-backed via idx_article_created_at, EXPLAIN plan unchanged) and have near-dup reuse that prefix, eliminating the separate content read entirely (two-query 92ms -> folded 51ms per 300 rows; end-to-end 339ms -> 241ms; MinHash CPU also ~10x smaller on the prefix). Wire reprints share the lede so the prefix carries the collapse signal; the disclosed limitation (a rewrite that changes only the opening may not collapse) is in the method string and collapse_prefix_chars is surfaced; OO_LATEST_NEARDUP_PREFIX_CHARS overrides (500 floor); the internal _body_prefix never leaks into the payload. Tests incl. a crisp window proof (tiny prefix -> distinct openings not folded; large prefix -> shared body folds). LESSON: on SQLCipher, reading ANY column at/after `content` decrypts the whole content overflow chain, and substr() does not skip it -- so bounding a second content read means FOLDING it into a scan that already pays that decrypt, not adding a prefixed query.

item: D2: job-ify the /all diagnostics export (field-test Item 10, 36+ min blocking)
status: shipped (draft PR #600 onto 0.1; backend verified py3.13; FULL suite green per item [D0 3144, D1 3149, D2 3153, D3+D4 3165, D5 3167 passed / 7 skipped / 0 failed]; mypy 127=baseline; ruff F,B + advisory clean; bandit -ll 0; single alembic head 5ea842778603 untouched; pre-push two-connection negative-space skeptics + an adversarial review agent)
summary: The synchronous GET /api/diagnostics/all runs every diagnostic serially -- measured 36+ min at scale, holding a threadpool thread the whole time and repeatedly blocking the event loop. Job-ified it via the generic src/jobs/background.py manager (ALPHA built for exactly this): POST /all-job starts a cancellable BACKGROUND job building the SAME members off the request thread, streaming the zip to a server-side file under data_dir()/diagnostics/ (atomic .part -> os.replace, so a cancelled/failed run never publishes a half-written archive), reporting per-member progress and keeping only the newest archive; GET /all-job/status reports state + per-member progress + the ready filename/size; GET /all-job/download serves the finished file (404 until a build completes). CONTRACT LESSON (the A2 defect, live at task time): the OLD synchronous GET /all is KEPT working during the transition (absorption-gated) -- a changed contract with an unwired UI mints false statements; EPSILON wires the new flow, the old route retires only after. Both paths build from ONE shared member list (_all_diagnostics_members) so they can never drift. Read-only job (is_writer=False, no write-gate contention); cancel is cooperative BETWEEN members (a single member can't be interrupted mid-run) so a finished-with-full-result run is never mislabelled cancelled. LESSON: job-ifying a long sync export must keep the old route until the UI is wired (absorption-gated) and share ONE member list so the sync + job archives are byte-identical.

item: D3: trending-windows serves the PREVIOUS rollup stale-but-disclosed during a rebuild (P1.10)
status: shipped (draft PR #600 onto 0.1; backend verified py3.13; FULL suite green per item [D0 3144, D1 3149, D2 3153, D3+D4 3165, D5 3167 passed / 7 skipped / 0 failed]; mypy 127=baseline; ruff F,B + advisory clean; bandit -ll 0; single alembic head 5ea842778603 untouched; pre-push two-connection negative-space skeptics + an adversarial review agent)
summary: trending-windows measured 467s/call at 20.9M mentions while the in-memory rollup was still building. INVESTIGATION: rollup_serve ALREADY serves the previous build during a stale rebuild (windowed_counts/windowed_rows return the current build when stale+have; only not-have falls to live) -- so the residual 467s is the FIRST build (no previous), which D3 correctly keeps on the live path. The gap was HONESTY + a missing regression guard: (a) basis() now discloses stale/rebuilding/age_seconds so a served-stale response is honestly labelled 'these are the PREVIOUS build's numbers, a rebuild is running'; (b) trending_windows (the Home poll) previously DROPPED each window's basis -- now propagates it to the top level so the staleness actually reaches the polled endpoint. NEGATIVE-SPACE / two-connection tests: serve-previous-during-rebuild returns the real previous-build numbers (identical to live, never a blend) with as_of visible + a rebuild triggered; a rollup built over one engine is NEVER served to a session on ANOTHER engine (the _same_bind guard, two engines); no-previous-build falls back to live (never a partial rollup answer); the basis disclosure itself. LESSON: a build-once-serve-many rollup already serves-stale-during-rebuild by construction; the risk is (1) the staleness never being disclosed and (2) an aggregator (trending_windows) silently dropping the per-part basis, so the polled endpoint shows numbers with no as_of.

item: D4: robust FTS presence probe -- fixes the present/absent contradiction (2026-07-09 zip)
status: shipped (draft PR #600 onto 0.1; backend verified py3.13; FULL suite green per item [D0 3144, D1 3149, D2 3153, D3+D4 3165, D5 3167 passed / 7 skipped / 0 failed]; mypy 127=baseline; ruff F,B + advisory clean; bandit -ll 0; single alembic head 5ea842778603 untouched; pre-push two-connection negative-space skeptics + an adversarial review agent)
summary: The 2026-07-09 diagnostics zip had corpus-integrity reporting article_fts ABSENT (fts_rows null) while schema-drift reported it PRESENT. ROOT CAUSE: integrity derived presence from `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM article_fts` -- on a large external-content FTS5 index that enumerates the whole index, can hit the shared statement deadline, and _scalar()'s broad except then returned None => 'present: False'. Meanwhile schema-drift reads sqlite_master (get_table_names) => present. FIX: new src/database/fts.py:fts_status(session) reads PRESENCE from sqlite_master (authoritative, deadline-proof, the SAME source schema-drift reads, so the two can't disagree), runs a cheap bounded health probe (SELECT rowid ... LIMIT 1) that surfaces post-crash MALFORMED-index corruption FAST as healthy=False (actionable: a re-index heals it), and attempts the row COUNT best-effort under its OWN bounded deadline reporting count_status ok|timed_out|error -- rows=None on a timeout is NEVER conflated with absence. integrity.py calls it OUTSIDE the shared counter-drift deadline (so nesting the progress-handler deadline can't clobber the outer one) and keeps the articles-count delta. NEGATIVE-SPACE tests: a simulated slow count -> present=True/rows=None/timed_out (the exact bug); a malformed-index error -> present=True/healthy=False/error; a genuinely absent table -> present=False; and integrity + schema-drift AGREE even when the count times out. LESSON: SQLite FTS5 COUNT(*) enumerates the index and can time out -- deriving table PRESENCE from a COUNT misreads a slow/corrupt index as absent; presence must come from sqlite_master (the schema), the count is a separate best-effort signal, and both probes must read presence from the same authoritative source so they never disagree.

item: D5: bind-qualify the generic _ckey TTL cache keys in insights (latent Wave-1 class)
status: shipped (draft PR #600 onto 0.1; backend verified py3.13; FULL suite green per item [D0 3144, D1 3149, D2 3153, D3+D4 3165, D5 3167 passed / 7 skipped / 0 failed]; mypy 127=baseline; ruff F,B + advisory clean; bandit -ll 0; single alembic head 5ea842778603 untouched; pre-push two-connection negative-space skeptics + an adversarial review agent)
summary: The insights TTL read-cache keyed payloads by _ckey(name, **params) with NO database qualifier, so a test fixture on its own engine and the live store computing the same query params could SHARE a cached payload (the latent Wave-1 class the audit flagged; cross-test cache pollution, the same family as the SessionLocal-seed lesson). FIX: a _bind_key(db, key) helper prefixes the key with id(db.get_bind()); _deadlined + the direct _cached(_ckey(...)) endpoints + the background warmer all route through it, so a cached insights payload is NEVER shared across databases. BYTE-IDENTICAL BEHAVIOUR on ONE engine (a constant prefix on every key -> same hit/miss). The unknown-bind fallback returns the UNqualified key (never a per-instance key), so the dummy-db (object()) unit tests still share correctly and the warmer<->endpoint keys still match for the same production engine (the P0-4 dead-warming trap avoided). Test: two different engines get distinct keys, one engine is stable, unknown-bind is unqualified-and-shared. LESSON: a process-global TTL cache keyed only by query params can serve one database's payload to a request on another (fixture-vs-live); bind-qualify the key with the engine id, but fall back to the UNqualified key on unknown bind so dummy-db tests + the background warmer (same engine) still match.

item: P0.5 scale-test harness -- synthetic corpus generator (G1) + benchmark runner with an honest JSON report (G2) + CI-safe opt-in scale-smoke tier (G3)
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.1; verified py3.13; FULL suite 3124 passed / 40 skipped / 0 failed; mypy 127=baseline; ruff full + F,B clean; bandit -r src -ll 0 Medium/High; scale_smoke 200MB run passes in ~49s; 5 adversarial negative-space skeptic lenses (determinism/schema-integrity/isolation/no-fabrication/caps-edges) run to completion pre-push -- FK check 0, counters==GROUP BY, 0 orphans, no-score walk empty, target-size + max-articles caps engage, encrypted unlock/wal + _guard verified)
key_paths: src/testing/__init__.py src/testing/corpus_gen.py src/testing/scale_bench.py scripts/generate_scale_corpus.py scripts/run_scale_bench.py tests/test_corpus_gen.py tests/test_scale_bench.py tests/scale/conftest.py tests/scale/test_scale_smoke.py
summary: P0.5 (the SCALE_ROADMAP structural gap: everything was verified at MB-GB, 100GB behaviour was discovered in the field). Converts the ATTENDED P0 backup/unlock/crash work into autonomous-VERIFIABLE work. G1 corpus_gen.py: a parameterized synthetic-corpus generator writing a REAL app-schema SQLite corpus of a target size, distributions grounded in the live 2026-07-09 numbers (268241 articles / 3.06M keywords / 20.9M mentions / 11.7GB; 71% single-article keywords). Schema from Base.metadata so it NEVER drifts from the models; FTS via the app's ensure_fts; keyword counters are the model's denormalised columns set EXACTLY from the generated mentions (zero drift, FK-clean, no orphans -- adversarially verified); a bounded Zipf head pool + a fresh single-article tail reproduce the real keyword shape; the ix_article_observed coalesce(published_at,created_at) EXPRESSION index the app self-heals at unlock is deliberately LEFT UNBUILT (create_all builds the model indexes; only that expression index is app-self-healed, not on any ORM model) so the bench's cold-unlock reproduces the field's 735s index-build cost. Exact --articles or grow-to --target-bytes; plaintext or SQLCipher; deterministic by seed (no wall-clock; byte-reproducible across PYTHONHASHSEED). Every corpus carries a VISIBLE synthetic marker (an app_state row + a SQLite application_id) so it is never mistakable for real data; is_synthetic() reads it back and degrades to None (never crashes) on a garbage/locked file. G2 scale_bench.py + run_scale_bench.py: measures unlock (cold init_db+ensure_* self-heals on a COLD COPY that builds ix_article_observed, then a WARM re-run = the P0.4 'is the next unlock fast?' discriminator; the bench engine mirrors the app PRAGMAs for timing fidelity), the REAL volumes+parity backup wall + a SAMPLED peak RSS (the field OOM) cross-checked vs getrusage ru_maxrss, the restore round-trip (verify + parity-recover), hot-endpoint p50/p95 (top/trending-windows/latest/graph/status via TestClient), and WAL growth under a commit-per-write burst (a BOUNDED peak = healthy auto-checkpoint hygiene, unbounded = the P0.3 runaway-WAL signal). ONE honest JSON report (report_schema oo-scale-bench-1; machine + corpus fingerprint + per-metric method) whose FORMAT IS the acceptance contract for the Round-2 (ZETA) backup rework -- run before + after, the report is the pass/fail evidence. Times/bytes/status ONLY, NO scores anywhere (no-score key-walk enforced in tests). unlock/wal are engine-mode (own engine on an explicit path, isolatable); backup/restore/endpoints are process-mode (OO_DATA_DIR set before importing the app; live_db_path + the real app), guarded so a phase failure degrades to {error} instead of aborting the report. G3 tests/scale/: an opt-in -m scale_smoke tier generating ~200MB (OO_SCALE_SMOKE_MB tunes) then running the full runner as a subprocess, asserting all 5 phases error-free + cold-builds-index/warm-faster + a backup volume + restore verified + endpoints 200 + no scores + minutes-not-hours (~49s at 200MB); skipped by default via a conftest marker guard so it never burdens the default lane. Isolated tmp_path engines only -- never SessionLocal / the shared OO_DATA_DIR. LESSON: to reproduce a field cold-unlock cost faithfully, generate the corpus WITHOUT the index the app self-heals at unlock -- create_all builds the model's __table_args__ indexes, so only the coalesce() EXPRESSION index (not reflectable, not on any model) is missing, and the bench's cold phase builds+times it while a warm re-run confirms the next unlock is fast. LESSON: an FTS-at-end 'rebuild' makes a target-size run overshoot after the last size check; build FTS INCREMENTALLY (ensure_fts BEFORE the fill so the triggers index per insert) to keep the measured size == the final footprint, and skip PRAGMA optimize/ANALYZE at generate time (a bulk-loaded corpus has no planner stats -- the unlock's optimize_at_boot builds them, part of the measured cost). LESSON: a POSITIVE-flag reader (is_synthetic) must wrap connect() itself, not just the row read, or an encrypted/garbage file RAISES instead of returning None -- found + fixed by the pre-push isolation/no-fabrication skeptic.

item: 12:14-boot log analysis folded into SCALE_ROADMAP: P0.4 one-time hypothesis REFUTED (unlock 1645 s recurring), map-serve flip P1.11, reconcile-cost P1.12, DELTA/EPSILON landings

item: rollup-serve reset fixture drains in-flight background builds (fix an order-dependent flake that merged via #600)
status: shipped (fresh follow-up branch from the merged 0.1; FULL suite green; ruff F,B clean; test-only, no production change; deterministic race reproducer proves the drain)
summary: The D3 test test_serves_previous_rollup_stale_but_disclosed_during_rebuild passed in isolation and within its own file but failed ONCE in a full-suite run (1 failed / 3188 passed), and #600 was merged before that rebased-tree suite finished (the merged != green hazard: the branch was force-pushed to clear a shipped.csv rebase conflict at the maintainer's request, and the still-running suite then surfaced the flake). ROOT CAUSE: rollup_serve._STATE is a PROCESS-GLOBAL singleton and its build runs on a DAEMON THREAD; the serve is auto-on whenever duckdb is available, so ANY test across the suite that runs a windowed top_terms/trending starts a real _build_and_swap that can still be in flight when a stale-serve test here runs -- the thread then swaps _STATE (con/bind/built_at) to a FRESH conftest-DB build, flipping the test's deliberately-STALE built_at to fresh so basis.stale reads False and the assertion fails. The old _reset_serve_state autouse fixture cleared _STATE only in TEARDOWN and never waited for the build thread, so the swap raced the test body. FIX: _reset_serve_state now DRAINS any in-flight build (waits out _BUILD_LOCK, which _trigger_build_async holds from trigger to completion) and clears the singleton BOTH before and after each test. VERIFIED deterministically: with a lingering build holding the lock and swapping _STATE after a 0.4s delay, the drain blocks ~0.4s until it completes, then the test's stale state holds. LESSON: a process-global singleton mutated by a DAEMON THREAD needs its test reset fixture to (a) run BEFORE as well as after each test and (b) DRAIN the in-flight worker (wait out its lock), not just clear the state -- clearing alone loses to a thread that swaps the state back mid-test; and force-pushing before the full suite CONFIRMS green (even to clear a docs conflict) can land a real flake when the base has moved.

item: Harness honesty fix-forward (post-merge audit of #601): determinism repro fixed + anti-flattering guards
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.1; 32 harness tests green; mypy 127; ruff clean; no migration)
summary: The post-merge audit of #601 (PASS with 3 conditions) found the harness could FLATTER a bad Round-2 backup: (V1, repro'd) _source_tags used a bare set join, so sources.tags order flipped with PYTHONHASHSEED — the byte-reproducible claim was false and the cross-process test fingerprinted only mentions+keywords (blind to the defective table); FIXED with sorted() + the fingerprint now covers sources. (V2) unlock/wal work_dir defaulted to tempfile.mkdtemp = /tmp = tmpfs ON THE QUBES FIELD MACHINE (the repo's own ledger lesson): a RAM-disk copy fakes I/O costs and ENOSPC at 50 GB; FIXED — the cold copy now defaults BESIDE the corpus (same filesystem), and the unlock method string now DISCLOSES page-cache warmth (the copy just wrote the file, so a RAM>corpus box understates the field's disk-I/O term — disclosed, not corrected). (V3) nothing warned when a report was built over a PLAINTEXT corpus although the field cost is DOMINATED by the SQLCipher codec; FIXED — run_full attaches a loud plaintext_caveat ('do NOT use for scale-acceptance; regenerate with --passphrase') whenever corpus.encrypted is false, with a regression test asserting the caveat present on plaintext AND absent on encrypted. The Round-2 acceptance conditions (assert report.corpus.encrypted == true; official backup number from a fresh-process --phases backup run to defeat the ru_maxrss delta-masking edge) are pinned in SCALE_ROADMAP's ROUND 2 section. LESSON (extends the negative-space rule): an acceptance INSTRUMENT needs its own adversarial audit — ask 'how could this gauge read green for a failing implementation' (plaintext-vs-encrypted, tmpfs, page-cache warmth, sampler masking), not just 'does it measure'.

item: WAL-switch race in dump_index._open (macOS observation lane): mode-check-first + never fail the build over the pragma
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.1; 13 dump tests green; full suite pre-push)
summary: The macOS observation lane hit sqlite3.OperationalError 'database is locked' on dump_index._open's PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL (test_dump_fts_search round-trip: build_index in a worker thread races the endpoint's reader; the mode switch needs a moment with NO other active connection). Same family as the custody-log fix. FIX: busy_timeout moves BEFORE the pragma (module constant _BUSY_TIMEOUT_MS so tests can shrink it); WAL is a PERSISTENT file property, so _open now reads the current mode LOCK-FREE and attempts the switch only when needed, wrapped so a locked-out switch degrades to rollback mode instead of failing the index build (the pragma is an optimization, never a correctness requirement — all DDL and real writes still raise loudly). Two deterministic tests: steady-state open beside an active reader on a WAL file, and the locked-out switch path (file forced back to DELETE mode + a SHARED-holding reader -> _open succeeds, honestly still rollback, fully usable). LEDGER NOTE: the observation-only macOS lane doing its designed job — catching the timing flake before the blocking lane.

item: THETA R2 — serve-by-default + rebuild-on-change: map serve flipped auto-on (P1.11) · change-gated rollup refresh (P1.10) · reconcile/prune deadlines + resumable watermarks (P1.12) · storage-composition diagnostic (P1.5) · noprobe-key + pollJobStatus riders
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.1; FULL suite green per item; mypy 127; ruff F,B; bandit -ll 0; alembic single head; node --check; i18n 100%)
key_paths: src/analytics/map_serve.py src/analytics/rollup_serve.py src/analytics/serve_gate.py src/analytics/store.py src/monitoring/storage.py src/api/insights.py src/api/diagnostics.py src/static/app.js
