item: P0.3 collector OOM fix (memory instrument+bounds, pass recycling, RSS memory guard, WAL checkpoint hygiene) + P1.8 collector write batching
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.1; commit per item; FULL suite green per item on py3.13 .venv; mypy 127; ruff F,B; bandit -ll clean; 3 adversarial skeptic lenses run to completion pre-push)
key_paths: src/scheduler/hygiene.py src/scheduler/memguard.py src/scheduler/runner.py src/ingest/batch.py src/ingest/pipeline.py src/ingest/crawl.py src/ingest/__init__.py src/monitoring/collect_perf.py src/api/scheduler.py src/testing/collect_soak.py tests/test_collect_memory_hygiene.py tests/test_pass_recycling.py tests/test_memory_guard.py tests/test_wal_checkpoint.py tests/test_collect_batching.py tests/test_collect_soak.py
summary: Field event 2026-07-09 (kernel OOM at RSS 10,599 MB on a ~10,237 MB VM, 21.6 h into ONE crawl pass, silent kill; 847,351 s cumulative writer-gate wait / 234,551 contentions / max single wait 438 s). E1: fetcher host caches bounded (robots-cap eviction = fail-closed recompute; last-request evicted only when >6 h old - politeness outranks the bound; host locks never evicted) + per-sample memory gauges + the RSS curve on every pass summary + between-pass release (trafilatura reset_caches, gc, glibc malloc_trim - measured before/after). E2: pass recycling - OO_PASS_BUDGET_MINUTES (default 60; 0=off) + OO_PASS_MAX_SOURCES; the un-run remainder defers and runs FIRST next pass; exactness pinned (processed+deferred==all). E3: RSS memory guard - psutil-measured trip/resume latch, hysteresis both ways (default RSS>=85% of RAM or avail<=256 MB x3 consecutive samples; missing readings never count), pauses loudly (phase paused-low-memory + status.memory_guard + perf samples), resumes on measured recovery or user action (start/run-now/POST /api/scheduler/memory-guard/resume), never touches the writer gate; conftest autouse latch-release fixture (process-global state, the pollution lesson). E4: PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) between passes via write_lock() (queues behind any writer - pinned by a held-gate test), rate-limited, bounded busy handler, honest busy=1 partial under an active reader, measured wal bytes before/after. E5/P1.8: collector write batching - fetch/extract/link-extraction OUTSIDE the gate (HTML dropped at stage; buffer bounded by count AND bytes), ONE transaction per batch via index_article(commit=False) + the proven rollback-then-redo-per-article fallback; zero loss pinned (Nth-article collision drops no batch-mate; death-between-commits re-ingests cleanly; live contention race with exact counters); OO_COLLECT_COMMIT_BATCH default 8, 0 = legacy exactly; measured ~16 gate windows -> 1 on an 8-article feed. BONUS ROOT-CAUSE (skeptic pass, reproduced empirically): feed bookkeeping before the article loop was AUTOFLUSHED by the loop's first dedup SELECT - the write gate was then held ACROSS article fetches (legacy: the whole first fetch of every first-contact feed = the 438 s max-single-wait signature; batched would hold the whole feed); fixed by end-of-loop bookkeeping + commit, gate-probe tests pin both paths + the sequential shared-session case. ACCEPTANCE SOAK (src/testing/collect_soak.py, zero network by construction - socket.socket is a TRAP in the smoke test): 6 recycled passes x 100 sources, RSS FLAT 232.5->235.7 MB (+3.2 MB after warm-up) while storing 2,300 articles; guard proven pause-not-die on injected fake readings (100 deferred, reason honest, pass returned in 0.01 s). See SHIPPED_LOG for the reusable autoflush-gate lesson.

item: P0.1 streaming backup engine (oo-volumes-2): no plaintext snapshot, no zip, bounded RAM, incremental, resumable, verifiable
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.1; Round 2 ZETA; 50+ backup tests green incl. negative-space skeptics; mypy 127; bandit 0; no migration)
key_paths: src/backup/stream_backup.py src/backup/parity.py src/backup/artifact.py src/backup/volume_job.py src/backup/volumes.py src/safety/crypto.py src/api/backup_v2.py src/testing/scale_bench.py scripts/run_scale_bench.py
summary: The 2026-07-09 field event: the volume backup materialized the corpus TWICE (plaintext snapshot = decrypt-the-world + an oo-backup-2 zip) and its Reed-Solomon parity then loaded the WHOLE volume set into RAM — guaranteed OOM at the field's 11.7 GB corpus on a 10 GB VM, on the very path meant to save the corpus. REPLACED the container: every member streams DIRECTLY into OOENC2 volumes; the corpus is never decrypted at backup time (the live file streams as its at-rest SQLCipher bytes inside one writer-gate window after a WAL checkpoint; a residual WAL rides as a member and folds back at restore); parity is banded ((M+1)x32 MiB, bytewise-identical output). INCREMENTAL: per-volume plaintext SHA-256 — only changed volumes re-emit (checksum never size/mtime; passphrase-bound via key_check; reuse itself ciphertext-verified). RESUMABLE = the same mechanism: an interrupted run leaves volumes.building.json and NO final manifest (never mistakable for complete; verify+restore refuse loudly); the next run re-hashes every slice against the CURRENT state in one gate window so every finished manifest is ONE consistent snapshot. CRASH-SAFE REFRESH: emitted volumes use run-unique names so a refresh never overwrites the previous complete set (atomic manifest swap + post-finalize GC) — an interrupted/cancelled refresh leaves the last good backup restorable. VERIFY (job + POST /api/backup/v2/volumes/verify): Ed25519-signed volume manifest + every data/parity checksum + structure without decrypting; with the passphrase a full stream-decrypt into a hash sink + signed-envelope cross-check; names exactly the bad volumes + parity recoverability. Restore converts SQLCipher corpus/custody members with the corpus's OWN passphrase (corpus_passphrase -> live key -> backup passphrase); additive merge byte-unchanged. Janitor: cleanup_stale_staging now sweeps .bak-build-*/.restore-*/*.oopart (age-guarded on the newest inner file + an active-job registry). GAMMA gate: acceptance_gate hard-fails a plaintext corpus, enforces supplied RSS bounds (never invents one), --official requires a fresh-process backup-only run; smoke tier now encrypted. Bench at the sandbox max (~6 GB encrypted synthetic; 30 GB disk cap): see the PR body for measured walls/RSS.

item: #606 macOS-lane fix-forward: storage-composition tests probe dbstat at runtime instead of assuming it
status: shipped (fresh branch from the merged 0.1 tip; storage tests green here; test-only)
summary: The macOS Portability-observation lane failed at #606's head SHA (merged before the lane finished — the standing merged!=green fast-merge reality): the two tests needing the FULL per-table dbstat split asserted available is True unconditionally, but SQLITE_ENABLE_DBSTAT_VTAB is a PER-BUILD flag — Linux stdlib sqlite3 has it, the macOS runner's Python build does NOT (and sqlcipher3 never does). The production code degraded exactly as designed ({available:false, reason} — never a 500); the TESTS were platform-naive. FIX: a runtime _dbstat_available() probe (stdlib sqlite3 in-memory SELECT against dbstat — the same library SQLAlchemy's sqlite dialect rides) gates the two full-split tests via skipif; the degrade tests keep running everywhere (on macOS the degrade path is exercised NATIVELY). CLAUDE.md + SHIPPED_LOG lesson refined from sqlcipher3-lacks-dbstat to dbstat-is-per-build-probe-it. LESSON: capability-gated tests must gate on a RUNTIME capability probe, never on the platform or the driver name.

item: Post-merge adversarial audit of the Round-2 wave: ZETA backup path hardened (traversal, crash-safe finalize, gate-disabled disclosure) + F4 400
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.1; backup/api/parity suites green [75 passed]; full suite pending)
summary: An 8-lens negative-space audit of the merged ZETA/ETA/THETA wave (suite green 3340) found crypto/snapshot/serve/memguard all GUARDED and a defect cluster on the backup finalize/restore path, fixed forward: F1 [HIGH] restore turned unvalidated corpus_member/wal_member manifest fields (+ per-member volume refs) into an arbitrary-file DELETE of the LIVE corpus from a self-signed hostile backup — _require_safe_manifest_names now covers every name->path field (restore AND verify call it); F5-F8 [HIGH data-loss] finalize wrote the new UNSIGNED manifest over the previous signed one before signing+parity, and an uncaught parity failure left it so cleanup_cancelled_build (unsigned=disposable) could delete a complete backup — finalize now builds the signed(+parity) manifest in memory and swaps dest/volumes.json in ONE atomic replace, so a crash/kill/parity-failure leaves the previous backup fully verifiable+restorable and no unsigned manifest ever lands at the canonical path (write_parity gained an opt-in in-memory mode + atomic legacy write); F12 [low] a loud note when OO_WRITE_GATE=0 voids the snapshot guarantee (never report writes-paused when it was not); F4 [nit] encrypted-store backup refusal returns 400 not 500. Deferred to SCALE_ROADMAP: F6 parity GF(2^8) N+M<256 ceiling (~128GB corpus / impossible at 5TB -> adaptive volume sizing, folds into P0.1), F13 batched-flush-holds-gate-across-extraction, F10/F11 backup-gate-hold, F3 restore-preflight-undercount, F14 markets-dirty-session. LESSON: every manifest/config field that becomes a filesystem path must pass the traversal guard, not just the obvious name fields; a finalize that overwrites the canonical artifact before the replacement is valid has a crash window that destroys the prior good artifact — build it complete, then swap atomically once; and a test double injected via a param (corpus_source) bypasses the production path, so a fix in the real path needs a test that exercises the real path.

item: B2: regression guard pinning the FRED 2-letter ISO convention for OECD share-price index ids
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.2; test-only; the config fix itself landed 2026-07-09)
summary: Field-test 2026-07-08 Item 1: the OECD SPASTT01<CC>M661N feed ids had used a 3-letter ISO code (SPASTT01DEUM661N) that does not exist on FRED, so every OECD index 404'd and Europe/Asia(ex-Nikkei)/Africa/S.America/Oceania boards showed empty. The config fix (ISO-3->ISO-2) already landed on 0.2 (2026-07-09), but no test pinned the convention. Added test_oecd_share_price_ids_use_the_fred_two_letter_iso_code: every 'oecd'-tagged feed's symbol must match ^SPASTT01[A-Z]{2}M661N$ AND appear verbatim in its fetch url (no symbol<->url drift), so a regression to 3-letter fails LOUDLY here instead of silently in the field. VERIFIED py3.13 venv: 3/3 test_index_catalog green (19 OECD feeds, all 2-letter, no drift). Loud-fail-on-wrong-id honesty preserved (feeds 404 rather than fabricate).

item: B1: offline zh/ja/th word segmentation via the [segmentation] extra (jieba/janome/pythainlp) + ko/mr stoplists (2a ruling executed)
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.2; py3.13 venv green; CI exercises it via the added [segmentation] extra; Core-only job proves graceful degrade)
key_paths: src/analytics/segmentation.py src/analytics/extract.py src/analytics/managed.py src/analytics/engine_report.py src/analytics/selftest.py src/services/stopwords.py scripts/build_stopwords.py configs/stopwords_iso/{zh,ja,th,ko,mr}.txt configs/external_artifacts.yml pyproject.toml .github/workflows/ci.yml tests/test_segmentation.py
summary: Maintainer delegated 2a: pick+ship license-clean offline zh/ja/th segmenters. CHOSEN: jieba (MIT, zh) + janome (Apache-2.0 bundled dict, ja) + pythainlp newmm (Apache-2.0, th) -- all pure-local, tokenize over dictionaries bundled IN THE WHEEL (NO model download, NO network; verified offline), pip-installable via a new [segmentation] extra (no repo vendoring -> no 100MB-rule/license duplication; no *_AS_OF so no registry entry needed per the freshness protocol). SEAM: new src/analytics/segmentation.py exposes segment(text,lang)->[(word,offset)]|None (jieba yields offsets directly; janome/pythainlp reconstructed via forward-cursor find, exact since surfaces concatenate to input) + segmenter_available(lang) (lightweight __import__ check, does NOT load dicts). Hooked into extract._terms(): when segment() returns tokens use them with a language-aware min_len=2 (CJK words are 2 chars: 中国/政策/経済 -- the Latin 3-char floor would drop them), else the byte-identical whitespace _WORD_RE path (min_len=_MIN_TERM_LEN=3). GRACEFUL DEGRADE by construction: extra absent OR OO_SEGMENTATION=0 -> segment()=None -> old whitespace tokenizer, zh/ja/th stay honestly 'unsegmented' (a core install is byte-unchanged; proven by test + the Core-only CI job). STATUS FLIP: managed.language_status() is segmenter-aware (zh/ja/th -> 'functional' only when a segmenter is present + the vendored stoplist applies); is_managed refactored to language_status==functional (byte-identical for existing langs); engine_report._lang_status routes through the ONE source of truth; ko(Hangul)+mr(Marathi) added to MANAGED_LANGUAGES (space-segmented, distinct scripts, now carry stopwords-iso lists). STOPLISTS vendored from stopwords-iso 0.7.1 (byte-identical to 0.7.0 for existing langs; sr/az stay honestly uncovered -- absent from stopwords-iso): zh 794 / ja 134 / th 116 / ko 679 / mr 99; STOPWORDS_ISO_AS_OF 2026-06->2026-07 + registry last_verified 2026-07-10 (external-freshness green). MEASURED junk reduction on fixtures: OFF the whitespace tokenizer minted whole SENTENCES as one keyword (中国政府今天宣布了新的经济政策 = one 'keyword') / Thai mark-fragments; ON it yields real REPEATING words (经济/政策/市场/影响, 経済/政府/市場, เศรษฐกิจ/รัฐบาล/ผลกระทบ). The corpus-level win is that real words RECUR across articles (Heaps beta drops from ~0.95), making aggregations meaningful. 3 selftest golden cases (segmentation_{zh,ja,th}, gated on segmenter_available like the lemma case) + tests/test_segmentation.py (graceful-degrade tests run everywhere; segmenter-present tests skip without the extra; adversarial empty/whitespace/emoji/mixed-script/4000-char inputs never crash, offsets validated). NEGATIVE-SPACE verified (skeptic pass): byte-identical fallback, offset fidelity, no import cycle/heavy status-check load, kill switch, mixed-script drops digits keeps Latin+CJK. RETROACTIVE PATH: a keyword-only re-index applies segmentation to an existing corpus. VERIFIED py3.13 venv: 55 seg/managed/report + 241 keyword/freshness/invariants green; ruff F/B clean; mypy 0 new in-file errors; selftest 46/46.
